root_text.sn46.5:1.2: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn46.5:1.6: Idha, bhikkhu, satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn46.5:1.7: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.5:1.8: Tassime satta bojjhaṅge bhāvayato kāmāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati, bhavāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati, avijjāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati.
root_text.sn46.5:1.10: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.

translation_text.sn46.5:1.2: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha … and asked him,
translation_text.sn46.5:1.4: How are the awakening factors defined?”
translation_text.sn46.5:1.5: “Mendicant, they’re called awakening factors because they lead to awakening.
translation_text.sn46.5:1.6: A mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn46.5:1.7: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.5:1.8: As they develop the seven awakening factors, their mind is freed from the defilements of sensuality, desire to be reborn, and ignorance.
translation_text.sn46.5:1.9: When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn46.5:1.10: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn46.5:1.11: They’re called awakening factors because they lead to awakening.”
